text: Iron-deficiency/chronic anemia was the initial presenting problem that prompted workup
transcript_quote: My blood kept coming up low — the anemia — and they went looking for the reason.

text: Colon cancer diagnosed after workup (initially a polyp, then a colonic tumor)
transcript_quote: First it was a polyp. Then it was the tumor in the colon.

text: Patient declined further cancer treatment, electing comfort-focused care over additional therapy
transcript_quote: More treatment buys me hospital time, not porch time.

text: Ischemic heart disease with abnormal cardiac and coronary imaging findings
transcript_quote: I see the ischemic heart disease in your history, and the imaging of your heart and coronary circulation showed abnormal findings.

text: Chronic angina (chest tightness) managed with nitroglycerin spray, occurring a couple times per week with exertion or emotional stress, resolving in a few minutes
transcript_quote: A couple of times a week, maybe. If I take the stairs too quickly or get worked up about something. It settles in a few minutes.

text: Dull, heavy lower abdominal pain intermittently, low intensity
transcript_quote: The belly gets dull and heavy sometimes, low down. Nothing that shouts.

text: Fatigue is the predominant symptom
transcript_quote: Tired is the main complaint. Tired is a heavy coat.

text: Decreased appetite and increased napping, attributed to cancer, heart disease, and anemia
transcript_quote: his appetite has gotten small, and he naps more than he used to

text: Clopidogrel 75 mg orally once daily continued unchanged
transcript_quote: Clopidogrel, 75 milligrams, once a day.

text: Simvastatin 20 mg orally at night continued unchanged
transcript_quote: Simvastatin, 20, at night.

text: Metoprolol (long-acting) 100 mg continued unchanged
transcript_quote: The long-acting metoprolol, 100 milligrams.

text: Nitroglycerin spray 0.4 mg sublingual as needed for chest pain continued unchanged
transcript_quote: the nitroglycerin spray, 0.4 milligrams under the tongue when the chest acts up

text: Plan to keep all medications unchanged for now; clopidogrel and simvastatin identified as candidates for future deprescribing if pill burden becomes difficult
transcript_quote: For today we keep everything exactly as it is — no changes while everything else is new — but over time, if swallowing a handful of pills becomes a chore, those are the ones we would retire first.

text: Decision to stop routine blood draws/lab monitoring unless results would change comfort
transcript_quote: We will not chase numbers anymore — no more blood draws unless they would change his comfort.

text: Hospice initial patient assessment performed
transcript_quote: Today is the initial assessment — I will listen to his heart and lungs, check his skin, look at safety around the house.

text: Individualized plan of care developed with patient's goals in his own words and visit schedule

slots.procedure: development of individualized plan of care

transcript_quote: From that we write his individualized plan of care: his goals in his own words, and the schedule.

text: Hospice care regime/therapy initiated
transcript_quote: the hospice admissions nurse

text: Hospice eligibility certification completed by two physicians (attending and hospice team physician)
transcript_quote: two physicians certify that the illness has reached the stage where comfort is the right medicine — his attending physician and I have completed that certification

text: Formal notifications sent to primary physician and Medicare
transcript_quote: we send the formal notifications — his primary doctor, Medicare — so the paperwork routes through us now

text: Nursing visits scheduled a couple of times per week, next visit Thursday
transcript_quote: I will visit a couple of times a week to begin.

text: Home health aide for bathing offered but declined by patient; documented as 'not yet' in plan
transcript_quote: An aide can come to help with bathing — PT: Not yet. A man can still run his own shower.

text: Chaplain and social worker services offered
transcript_quote: There is also a chaplain, and a social worker, if either of you ever wants a longer conversation.

text: Counseled to call the 24-hour hospice line first for symptoms/emergencies (e.g., chest pain) rather than calling an ambulance
transcript_quote: Chest pain at three in the morning — you call us first, not the ambulance.

text: Respite care benefit explained to support caregiver
transcript_quote: there is respite built into this benefit — if you ever need a few days to rest, we arrange his care so you can step away

text: Patient expressed goals of care: DNR/DNI — no resuscitation, intubation, chest compressions, or emergency transport
transcript_quote: Nothing heroic. No machines, no pounding on my chest, no sirens in the driveway.

text: Patient's stated goals of a good day: time on porch, listening to jazz records, wife's cooking, and quiet — care to be built around these
transcript_quote: The porch in the morning if it is not too cold. My records in the afternoon... Her cooking. And quiet.

text: Social isolation: patient has a very small social circle and often goes weeks without seeing anyone besides his wife; content with this
transcript_quote: He would go weeks without seeing a soul besides me. But he is content that way

text: Patient lives at home with wife of 51 years who serves as primary caregiver and manages his medications
transcript_quote: Margaret loads the box every Sunday. I am the talent; she is management.

text: Caregiver instructed to keep a comfort diary tracking pain, appetite, and sleep between visits
transcript_quote: I will show you how to keep a little diary of his comfort — pain, appetite, sleep — so every visit starts from what the week actually looked like

text: History and physical note documented for encounter
Symptom-focused management plan to treat pain, breathlessness, chest tightness, and anxiety
transcript_quote: we treat what he feels: pain, breathlessness, chest tightness, worry
